transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log for the 31st of August 2020.

I was sleeping.

So it was like 8 hours of sleep, 6 to 2.
But 8 hours of sleep, 8 hours down, so 16 hours of work, solid,
Except for like an hour when I went to my mom's house.
Yeah, and today, yeah, maybe 6 to 2 to, and it's 5 now, so.
So, 2 is 2 is 12, plus 3, 15.
I've been working 15 hours.
Should probably maybe have a break.
So, yeah, I'm just doing like, I'm pulling out all the stops for Atlantis.
So, yeah, I did some other creative things like, you know,
I finished the things that I needed to do.

Which was drawing in the islands.
I decided to put islands inside of the lakes.
I have the lakes scattered throughout the city.
And inside of the islands, lakes, I put islands that are the size of the Olympic pool
Here in Boulder, Scott, the Carpenter Park Pool.
And then I put that pool next to the lake or next to the track
And these little town centers divided from the city.
So, then I color-coded, divided those town centers in the city,
Of which there were 32.
I made 32.
30, I mean 31.
30 of the ones that go around.
18 on the inner, outer right ring, and 12 on the inner.
I wanted to make it look natural, so I had it like four different types of green.
The number one was its own green, and then the other one's going around.
Shared between four others.
One of them's not really a green, but,
Yeah, I wanted the balance between making it look natural,
But also you could see the differences of them.
And it's kind of more difficult when you have to see through the actual buildings.
But I think have a good balance.
I wanted to divide the different neighborhoods in it to know how big they were.
Exactly how much area a certain town center occupied,
And how many people it does, and how much space it takes up.
I haven't gotten around to taking up the space,
But I did calculate the population of the city the first time.
I used the screen capture of the apartments of Pellegrini in ancient Rome,
Year 2 to 211.
And for some reason, I never counted.
Like, I didn't have the count on the image, or I didn't remember.
So I just now counted the population of each apartment of Pellegrini,
Just by counting the number of apartments in there,
Not including this one central area,
Which I just call, like, the community area,
The communal area of the apartment.
It's 310 people each, and there was, like, 1,711.
You know, 7,118 of those buildings that I have in that city.
So that means 2.2 million people.
I thought it would be a million, but...
Because ancient Rome at that size was a million,
So this is double the density.
So, yeah.
I think maybe some people said it's 2 million.
I don't know.
Maybe it's kind of believable.

Because they're, like, family houses and so.

The island,
